test_name: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x)=25x+30$. What is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=2$?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. It’s given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=25x+30$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in this equation yields $f\left(2\right)=25\left(2\right)+30$, which is equivalent to $f\left(2\right)=50+30$, or $f\left(2\right)=80$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(x\right)$ is $80$ when $x=2$.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $25\left(2\right)$, not $25\left(2\right)+30$.
Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $25+2+30$, not $25\left(2\right)+30$.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $\left(25+30\right)\left(2\right)$, not $25\left(2\right)+30$.

If one of these colonies has 41 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage: The graph models the number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six-month period, where $x$ is the number of six-month periods since the end of January 1995 and $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the point $(1, 500)$ in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 500.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers increased every six months by 500 subscribers.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1995 was 500.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January was 500.

test_name: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$f(x) = 7,000(1.005)^{2x}$
The given function $f$ models the balance of a bank account, in dollars, $x$ years after it is opened. Which statement is the best interpretation of $(1.005)^{2x}$?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Every 6 months, the balance increases by about \$5.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Every 2 months, the balance increases by about \$5.

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval.

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the end of every 2-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 2-month interval.
